subject: English
chapter: The Case of the Fifth Word
source_pdf: The Case of the Fifth Word.pdf

key_points: Encyclopedia Brown, whose real name is Leroy Brown, is a clever boy who helps his father solve difficult mysteries
His father, Chief Brown, often shares unsolved police cases with him during dinner discussions
Tim Nolan, who once lived in Idaville, was suspected of taking part in a jewellery robbery with Daniel Davenport
Before dying, Nolan left a coded message on a calendar sheet containing four strange words
Encyclopedia Brown decoded the message and discovered the location where the stolen jewellery was hidden
examples: Chief Brown brought home the mystery of Nolan’s death and the strange message
The four words written by Nolan were Nom, Utes, Sweden, and Hurts
These words were actually clues representing Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday
The missing fifth word represented Friday
The hidden jewellery was discovered under a young fir tree in Nolan’s nursery
formulas_or_processes: Problem → Mystery of the stolen jewellery and Nolan’s coded message
Clue → Four unusual words written on a calendar sheet
Observation → Encyclopedia realizes the words represent days of the week
Inference → The missing fifth word corresponds to Friday
Solution → The clue points to the fir tree where the jewellery was hidden
exam_tips: Remember the role of Encyclopedia Brown as a young detective with excellent reasoning skills
Understand how the calendar sheet helped identify the pattern of days
Focus on the logic behind removing the word 'day' from the days of the week to form the coded words
Be clear about how the missing fifth word helped reveal the hiding place of the jewellery
